question_text: John has just graduated from college with a business degree, but he has always been passionate about wildlife conservation. His parents, who paid for his expensive education, expect him to take a well-paying job in corporate sector, but he wants to work for a non-profit organization dedicated to wildlife conservation. Should John go against his parents' wishes to pursue his passion, potentially risking their disappointment and his financial stability?
